risk_title: Ongoing conflict affecting project implementation in multiple areas
risk_summary: The project continues implementation in conflict-affected areas, requiring specific monitoring and mitigation measures to be documented in financial reports.
evidence_quote: The PMU should include a separate note in quarterly IFRs detailing the impact of conflict and proposed mitigation measures, given the continued implementation in conflict areas.

risk_title: Corridor development project overlap creating safeguards complications
risk_summary: Government-led corridor development project in Addis Ababa overlaps with project interventions, requiring addendum RAPs and creating implementation risks that must be proactively managed.
evidence_quote: Any overlap between the project interventions and the government-led corridor development project in Addis Ababa must be notified to the World Bank team immediately, with proactive mitigation measures.

risk_title: Safeguards compliance delays in Dessie FSTP due to unresolved requirements
risk_summary: Construction of Dessie FSTP has been pending since 2023 awaiting completion of safeguards requirements compliance, indicating governance and coordination challenges.
evidence_quote: In Dessie town, construction has been pending since 2023, awaiting the completion of requirements to comply with safeguards requirements.

risk_title: Coordination gaps between government implementation agencies
risk_summary: Coordination between implementation agencies (MoWE and AAWSA) needs strengthening, with staffing vacancies at town and regional levels not yet addressed, affecting project delivery.
evidence_quote: Coordination between implementation agencies needs strengthening. However, the staffing vacancies at the town and regional levels have not been addressed. MoWE should promptly fill the necessary staff positions in consultation with the regions.

risk_title: Weak contract management and governance affecting multiple FSTP implementations
risk_summary: Pattern of contract extensions in all ongoing FSTP contracts and discrepancies between physical and financial progress indicate serious contract management and governance issues requiring immediate intervention.
evidence_quote: a pattern of contract extensions was noted in all the ongoing Fecal Sludge Treatment Plants (FSTPs) contracts. Discrepancies between physical and financial progress also points to construction and contract management issues. These findings pose serious concerns.
